title: Wikstroemia modesta
intro: Wikstroemia modesta, also known as the Tahitian cheese flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Wikstroemia modesta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Wikstroemia modesta is commonly prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is used to treat fever, skin conditions, and wounds.
taxonomy: Wikstroemia modesta is classified within the family Thymelaeaceae. This family comprises a total of 40 genera. As a member of the family Thymelaeaceae, Wikstroemia modesta is part of the order Thymelaeales. The order Thymelaeales is a part of the clade Asterids within the larger clade Angiosperms. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
